Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter (in person): Judith A. DeAlba, CSR #5709, Tel. 650.571.1473, Email: judy@dealbareportingservice.com. Bailiffs: Guitron/Fung/Gomez. (204)

Respondent, Martha Wash?S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner, Tanya Rockett?S Second Amended Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To Estate
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances in-person:
Ann K. Kavanagh, Esq. of JKZ LLP, Tel. 415.693.0550, for Martha Wash;
Milla L. Lvovich, Esq. of Lvovich Szucsko LLP, Tel. 415.392.2560 ext.111, for Tanya Rockett.

Matter contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Before the court is Martha Wash's Demurrer to Second Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate ("Demurrer").
Movant's request for judicial notice of various pleadings and documents filed in this matter is GRANTED, pursuant to Evid. Code secs. 452 and 453.

The Demurrer is IN PART OVERRULED AND IN PART SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

Movant previously demurred to Petitioner's Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate. On November 25, 2025, this court entered Order Sustaining In Part And Overruling In Part Demurrer To Amended Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To The Estate. Petitioner timely filed Second Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate.
Per the Notice of Demurrer, Movant now demurs to the Second Amended Petition ("SAP") under "California Code of Civil Procedure [secs.] 430.10(d), 430.10(e), and 430.10(f) as the Amended Petition contains a defect of parties, does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action, and it is uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible. Petitioner's verified admissions bar recovery under the causes of action pled." The Notice of Demurrer now before the court is brought, verbatim, on the same grounds as the previous demurrer, which was sustained in part with leave to amend and overruled in part.
Per the current Memorandum of Points and Authorities, Movant argues,
The Court must sustain Respondent's demurrers because Petitioner has: (a) failed to set forth any new facts not alleged in her [First Amended Petition], and, aside from one inapplicable code section, has failed to set forth any new legal argument not previously stated in her FAP or her opposition to Respondent's previous Demurrer to the FAP; (b) failed to state facts sufficient to constitute any cause of action ("COA") and failed to plead all the necessary elements of each of her COAs (Code Civ. Proc., [sec.] 430.10(e)); and (c) each of the "COAs" are so unclear, uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible that Respondent cannot reasonably respond (Id., [sec.] 430.10(f)).
Broadly speaking, Petitioner's arguments fail because: (1) they are contradicted by her judicial admissions and prior stipulations; (2) the law she cites is inapplicable to the claims she is trying to make; and/or (3) she cannot allege the facts necessary to establish the elements of her claims.
MPA at 2:2-11.
Movant also makes several assertions in footnotes as to why the prior demurrer was sustained, which are not reflected in the November 2025 Order or Minutes of the hearing.

[Part 1 of 3, mini minutes continue in Part 2 of 3]

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
[Part 2 of 3, mini minutes continue from previous entry]

When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication, cannot consider the party's ability to prove their factual allegations (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.; Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) Cal.4th 992, 998, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) Demurrers test legal sufficiency based on the allegations as pleaded, without regard to the plaintiff's ability to prove the alleged facts. (see, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) Demurrers challenge only the legal sufficiency of pleadings on their face, not the truth or adequacy of factual allegations or evidence (Code of Civil Proc. sec. 430.30(a)). The sole question raised by demurrer is whether the pleading is facially-not factually-deficient.
Movant's general demurrer on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure sec. 430.10(d), a defect or misjoinder of parties, is OVERRULED. The prior demurrer was sustained on this basis with leave to amend and the SAP now alleges the capacities in which Movant is named as a respondent.
Movant's general demurrer under Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(f) for uncertainty is OVERRULED. As the court ruled on the previous demurrer to the pleadings on these grounds, the Petition is sufficiently pleaded to apprise Movant of the allegations being made against her, in her capacities as pleaded, and the relief sought. (See, Butler v. Sequeira (1950) 100 C.A.2d 143, 145-146.)

Demur to First Cause of Action brought under Probate Code section 850(a)(2)) and seeking return of funds to the Estate, on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is OVERRULED. Although the pleading contains legal argument and conclusions, the pleading also states material facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action.
Demur to Second Cause of Action brought under Probate Code section 13111(a) and seeking return of funds to the Estate, on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is OVERRULED. Although the pleading contains legal argument and conclusions, the pleading also states material facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action.
Demur to Third Cause of Action seeking treble damages under Probate Code section 13111(b), on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Under the statute, "[i]f the transferee fraudulently secured the payment, delivery, or transfer of the decedent's property under this chapter, the transferee is liable under this section for restitution of three times the fair market value of the transferred property." (Probate Code sec. 13111(b). Facially, the pleading states, "Absent more information, Petitioner does not contend that Martha secured the AIG Funds belonging to the Estate by fraud." (SAP at par. 53.) Instead, the pleading alleges Respondent fraudulently retained the estate assets after the personal administrator requested transfer of those estate assets. Facially, this is not an adequate pleading of a claim pursuant to Probate Code section 13111(b). If Petitioner has since obtained information sufficient to allege Respondent secured the payment, delivery, or transfer of estate assets by fraud, Petitioner may, with specificity, do so in a further amended pleading.

(Part 2 of 3, mini minutes continue in Part 3 of 3)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ann Kavanagh for respondent and demurring party, Martha Wash.
Milla Lvovich for petitioner and opposing party, Tanya Rockett.

The matter was called for hearing on Respondent Martha Wash's Demurrer to Petitioner's Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to Estate.

Counsel for both sides presented oral argument regarding the Court's tentative ruling, which overruled the demurrer in part and sustained it in part with leave to amend. Ms. Kavanagh contested the Court's decision to overrule the demurrer as to the fourth cause of action. Ms. Lvovich requested clarification regarding the specific grounds on which the Court sustained the demurrer with leave to amend, so that she could appropriately address those issues in the amended petition.

After hearing argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PES-22-305427, The Estate of Gloria Jean Wash Riley
PTR-23-306673, In the Matter of Perry L and Gloria J. Wash Family Trust
PTR-24-307669, In the Matter of the Survivor's Trust of the Perry L. and Gloria J. Wash Family Trust

Appearances (via Zoom):
Milla Lvovich for and with Tanya Rockett, Executor/Respondent.
Nathan D. Borris for Partha E. Wash, Trusttee/Petitioner.

Discussion was held regarding estate matter 305427. Ms. Lvovich informed the court that she recently filed a Petition for Approval of Status Report and Request for Extension of Time to Continue Administration of the Estate, which is set for hearing on February 24, 2025. Counsel apologized to the court for missing the prior hearing and provided an explanation which the court found satisfactory. The court ordered the Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause off calendar and decided not to impose sanctions. The status on closing of estate hearing was also taken of calendar.

Further discussions were held in the trust matters (306673 and 307669). The court inquired about readiness for trial, case management, and discovery. Both counsel agreed there are no significant factual disputes, with issues primarily revolving around legal interpretations of trust documents. Counsel suggested the case may proceed based on briefing and oral argument without evidentiary hearings.

The court scheduled a court bench trial for January 17, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel were ordered to submit briefing and stipulated facts by January 10, 2025.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nathan D. Borris for Martha Wash, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court noted the absence of Milla L. Lvovich, counsel for executor Tanya Rockett. Mr. Borris explained that two trust-related matters are scheduled for hearing on Wednesday, December 4, 2024, which may significantly impact the estate proceedings. The court ordered the status on close of estate hearing to December 4, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 to coincide with the trust hearings. Additionally, the court will issue and order to appear to Ms. Lvovich and Order to Show Cause why sanctions in the amount of $250 should not be imposed for her failure to attend this morning's hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Nathan Borris appearing via zoom for and with Martha Wash appearing zoom.
Eric Stone appearing via zoom for and with Tanya Rockett (Petitioner) appearing via zoom.

Mrs. Borris stated that he waives direct service of notice on behalf of Marsha Wash and that his client concedes to Mrs. Rockett's appointment. Further discussion was held regarding concerns with ongoing delays from Mrs. Rockett, assets being placed in a blocked account, whether the proposed bond amount is sufficient to protect the beneficiaries, etc. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated February 21, 1996 and the codicil dated April 07, 2006 are admitted to probate. Tanya Rockett is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $162,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for June 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Shirley Yawitz is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge; Ross Moody; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Choi; Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-09-2022 Continued To Jan-04-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Borris, via Zoom, for Martha Wash, Petitioner; Eric Stone, via Zoom, for Tanya Rockett, Objector.

The court confirms with Mr. Borris that it has been determined that the gross value of the assets subject to probate falls below the small estate threshold and that the petition was filed to preserve his client's creditor's claims. Mr. Stone offers advocacy on behalf of his client's objection stating that the deadline for Petitioner or any other interested party to file an action to contest the terms of the Trust lapsed on February 26, 2022. Counsel presented additional oral argument to support their positions with regard to the instant petition. The court mentions probate code 8420, the statute of limitations and that venue needs to be confirmed. Mr. Stone requests a continuance to file a competing petition. Mr. Borris does not object to Mr. Stone's request. The court notes that Mr. Stone's client may be in the position to nominate the appointment of a third-party neutral as Administrator. Mr. Stone is instructed to include the date of the next calling on his petition and to indicate on the petition that Judge Moody has approved the setting of said petition on that same date.

The matter is continued to Jan-04-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Rice. Not Reported.
